title: GIF Metadata
description: Metadata contract for a single cataloged GIF. One file per GIF lives in metadata/{id}.json and mirrors an entry in index.json.

id.description: Short unique hash, e.g. 'a1b2c3d4'. Also the GIF filename stem.

source_id.description: Original GIPHY id, if applicable. Used for idempotency (never download the same source_id twice).

category.description: One of the level-1 folders (structural taxonomy).

subcategory.description: The level-2 folder within the category.

tags.description: Meaning / keyword list. Must come from schema/tags-controlled.json to avoid variants.

primary_emotion.description: The single dominant emotion the GIF conveys.

recommended_use.description: Short phrase: when to use it in a presentation (e.g. 'after showing a positive metric').

avoid_if.description: Optional context where this GIF would be inappropriate.

content_rating.description: GIPHY content rating filter, to keep assets corporate-safe.

duration_seconds.description: Playback duration in seconds (may be null when unknown).

brand_compatible.description: Optional list of brands/clients this GIF was validated against.

license_note.description: Note about GIPHY usage terms for this asset (see README section 5).

date_added.description: ISO date the asset was added.

review_pending.description: True until a human confirms category, tags and tone. GIFs with true MUST be excluded by consuming agents.
